acquisition method: Data-independent acquisition (DIA) is an increasingly used approach for quantitative proteomics.
alkylation reagent: Carbamidomethylation (+57.02) on cysteine and dimethylation (+28.03) on the N-terminus were set as fixed modifications
collision energy: Neutral loss of Ac-Ile is complete, and peptide backbone fragmentation is efficient at a NCE of 30
fragmentation method: the isotopically labeled Ac-Ile part of the tag leaves as a neutral loss fragment upon CID
instrument: making the method accessible to most high-resolution mass spectrometers.
labeling: the Ac-IP tag has a number of features that make it particularly suitable for multiplex DIA.
